title: The Art of Labeling
concept: Data labeling is the process of marking images with correct target tags so models can learn from them.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is data leakage in machine learning?

options.text: When test set data is accidentally exposed to the model during training, inflating score expectations
When raw image pixels leak out of GPU memory into the CPU
When annotations are saved in text format instead of JSON

exercises.explain: Data leakage occurs when target information leaks from test or validation sets into the training dataset.

cards.title: The Invisible Lab
cards.body: Before any modern neural network can tell a cat from a dog, human annotators had to draw millions of lines, click millions of boxes, and tag millions of images. High-quality data labeling is the foundation of modern AI.

cards.title: Data Labeling
cards.body: Data Labeling (or annotation) is the process of identifying raw data (like images) and adding one or more informative labels to provide context, allowing a machine learning model to learn from those examples.

cards.title: Coloring inside the lines
cards.body: Think of an unlabeled image as a blank coloring page. Human annotators are like artists who carefully color different shapes and write labels on each section (e.g., 'this is water', 'this is a house'). The AI then studies this completed drawing to learn.

cards.title: Label Types
cards.body: Different tasks require different labeling styles: a simple classification task needs a single string tag ('Car'). An object detection task needs four coordinates [x, y, w, h] (bounding box). Segmentation needs polygon outlines.

cards.title: AI cannot label itself from scratch
cards.body: You might think AI models can automatically label all data. In reality, models can generate draft labels, but they still rely on human verifiers to catch errors. Raw ground-truth data always starts with human eyes.

cards.title: Annotation Types
cards.body: Comparing annotation formats for different tasks:

left.text:
Rectangular coordinate box.
Stores top-left, width, height.
Fast to draw, lower detail.

right.text:
Detailed polygon boundaries.
Traces the exact outline of the object.
Slow to draw, very high detail.

exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of data labeling in computer vision?
exercises.explain: Labeling adds annotations that serve as ground truth, translating raw pixels into concepts the model can learn.

options.text: To add semantic meaning (like classes or outlines) to raw pixels so models can learn
To reduce the file size of images stored on disk
To automatically adjust the model's weights on a GPU
To double the camera's resolution during inference

exercises.prompt: True or False: Drawing a detailed segmentation mask is faster than drawing a standard bounding box.
exercises.explain: False. Segmentation masks require tracing exact polygons, taking significantly more time than drawing simple rectangular bounding boxes.
Bounding boxes are the standard rectangular annotations used to locate objects in object detection.
exercises.sentence: Object detection tasks require drawing a rectangular shape called a ___.

exercises.prompt: Which task requires the most detailed human annotation?
exercises.explain: Instance segmentation requires fine-grained polygon coordinates, which are time-consuming to create.

options.text: Image classification (assigning a single tag to the image)
Image resizing (changing image height and width)

exercises.prompt: You are managing a data labeling team for a retail checkout scanner. What strategy will save the most budget while maintaining quality?

options.text: Have annotators label classification tags first, then use bounding boxes only for the items that the classifier struggles with.
Require pixel-perfect polygon segmentation masks for every single image, even if you only need classification.
Discard human annotators and label all images using random guesses.
Scale down all images to 1x1 pixels to make labeling instant.

exercises.explain: Using the simplest labeling type that fits your task constraints prevents budget waste. Bounding boxes or polygon masks are overkill if classification is all you need.
